title: Feature Request: S-Curve Widget for Project Progress Tracking

posts.author: Gaabs (Gabriela Viveiros)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

I would like to check the possibility of monday evaluating the development of an improvement, which would be an S-Curve widget.

The S-Curve chart, in project management, is a visual representation that shows the cumulative progress of a project over time, comparing the planned versus the actual, both in terms of costs and physical effort.

The “S” shape of the chart comes from the slow initial progress, gradual acceleration, and then deceleration as the project approaches completion.

It would be very valuable to have this type of chart available in monday, displaying the data by month, and also allowing us to look ahead into future months. The idea is to clearly see the difference between the planned and the actual in both current and future timelines.

What are you trying to achieve

I tried to build an S-Curve chart using formulas, but it only shows the progress as tasks are completed, not the future projection. For the PMO, this view is not sufficient — they need to have visibility of planned vs. actual progress, including future months, in order to clearly see where the curve is forming.

It is also important that the PMO can identify and work around this by tracking the critical path, which is not possible with the current setup.

posts.author: jameswood32 (James Wood )
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for this feature! An S-Curve widget would be a great tool for tracking project progress visually. It could help compare planned vs. actual progress over time, making it easier to spot delays or performance issues early. This would be especially useful for project managers working on complex or long-term projects. Ideally, it should integrate with existing task or milestone data and support filtering by team, phase, or timeline. Hope this gets added to the roadmap!
